  {"source": "Ridge", "targets": {"zh-CN": "еІӯеӣһеҪ’"}, "category": "Statistics/ML", "do_not_translate": false, "context": "A type of linear regression that includes an L2 regularization term to address multicollinearity and variance inflation."},
  {"source": "Elastic Net", "targets": {"zh-CN": "еј№жҖ§зҪ‘з»ңеӣһеҪ’"}, "category": "Statistics/ML", "do_not_translate": false, "context": "A regularized regression method that combines the penalties of LASSO and Ridge regression."},
  {"source": "Akaike's Information Criterion (AIC)", "targets": {"zh-CN": "иөӨжұ дҝЎжҒҜеҮҶеҲҷ"}, "category": "Statistics", "do_not_translate": false, "context": "A measure used for model selection that penalizes the complexity of the model to prevent overfitting."},
  {"source": "Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC)", "targets": {"zh-CN": "иҙқеҸ¶ж–ҜдҝЎжҒҜеҮҶеҲҷ"}, "category": "Statistics", "do_not_translate": false, "context": "A criterion for model selection among a finite set of models, imposing a heavier penalty for model complexity compared to AIC."},
  {"source": "Multi-collinearity", "targets": {"zh-CN": "еӨҡйҮҚе…ұзәҝжҖ§"}, "category": "Statistics", "do_not_translate": false, "context": "A statistical phenomenon in which two or more predictor variables in a regression model are highly correlated, making it difficult to estimate the relationship between predictors and the dependent variable."},
  {"source": "Loss Function", "targets": {"zh-CN": "жҚҹеӨұеҮҪж•°"}, "category": "ML", "do_not_translate": false, "context": "A function that maps the prediction error of a model to a real number, representing the cost associated with the error."},
  {"source": "Dantzig Selector", "targets": {"zh-CN": "дё№йҪҗж јйҖүжӢ©еҷЁ"}, "category": "Statistics/ML", "do_not_translate": false, "context": "A statistical method used for variable selection and regularization in regression models."},
